title: Fall of the Berlin Wall
content: The Berlin Wall fell on November 9, 1989. It had divided East and West Berlin since August 13, 1961. The wall's fall was triggered by a miscommunicated press conference by East German spokesman Günter Schabowski, who announced that border crossings would be allowed 'immediately, without delay.'

title: Founding of the United Nations
content: The United Nations was established on October 24, 1945, when its Charter came into force. It was founded by 51 member states. The UN replaced the League of Nations, which had failed to prevent World War II. The headquarters is in New York City.

title: French Revolution
content: The French Revolution began on July 14, 1789, with the storming of the Bastille. It led to the abolition of the monarchy in 1792 and the execution of King Louis XVI in January 1793. The Revolution ended with Napoleon Bonaparte's coup d'état on November 9, 1799.

title: Magna Carta
content: The Magna Carta was signed by King John of England on June 15, 1215, at Runnymede. It established the principle that everyone, including the king, was subject to the law. It influenced the development of constitutional law worldwide, including the US Constitution.

title: Industrial Revolution
content: The Industrial Revolution began in Britain around 1760 and lasted until about 1840. Key inventions included James Watt's improved steam engine (1769), the spinning jenny (1764), and the power loom (1785). It spread to Continental Europe and North America in the early 19th century.

title: Sydney Opera House
content: The Sydney Opera House was designed by Danish architect Jørn Utzon. Construction began in 1959 and was completed in 1973. Utzon won the design competition in 1957 out of 233 entries. He was born in Copenhagen, Denmark in 1918. The building was designated a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2007.

title: Population of Copenhagen
content: Copenhagen, the capital of Denmark, has a city population of approximately 800,000 and a metropolitan area population of about 2.1 million as of 2023. It is the most populous city in Denmark and the seat of the Danish government.

title: Einstein's Birth
content: Albert Einstein was born on March 14, 1879, in Ulm, in the Kingdom of Württemberg in the German Empire. His family moved to Munich when he was an infant. He later renounced his German citizenship in 1896 and became a Swiss citizen in 1901.

title: Yao Ming Biography
content: Yao Ming was born on September 12, 1980, in Shanghai, China. He played for the Houston Rockets in the NBA from 2002 to 2011. At 7 feet 6 inches (2.29 m), he was one of the tallest players in NBA history. He became chairman of the Chinese Basketball Association in 2017.

title: Population of Shanghai
content: Shanghai is the most populous city in China with approximately 24.87 million permanent residents as of 2023. It is a global financial center and the world's busiest container port. The city covers 6,340.5 square kilometers.
